title_original: CUDA-Q Accelerates Quantum Workloads at GTC 2026 - NVIDIA Quantum
summary_original: At GTC 2026, the NVIDIA CUDA-Q platform showcased significant advances in GPU-accelerated quantum computing. CINECA and Kipu Quantum ran a 43-qubit statevector simulation of a Quantum-Enhanced Memetic Tabu Search algorithm on 2,048 NVIDIA Ampere GPUs. Infleqtion utilized 24,000 NVIDIA A100 GPU-node-hours for quantum neural networks in cancer biomarker discovery. Collaborations with institutions like UCL, QMatter, Technical University of Munich, and IQM demonstrated hybrid biomolecular simulations using the CUDA-Q platform and quantum hardware. Researchers at University of Edinburgh achieved a 900-fold speedup in quantum error correction decoding on NVIDIA GH200 GPUs. Multiple organizations—including Aegiq, Classiq, and Kvantify—demonstrated tangible simulation and workflow enhancements via CUDA-Q, marking notable progress in hybrid quantum-classical computing.
